question: What is Vue 3?

answers.text: A progressive JavaScript framework built with TypeScript

question: How do you create a Vue 3 application?

answers.text: Using createApp() instead of new Vue()

question: What is the Composition API in Vue 3?

answers.text: A new way to organize component logic using setup() and composables

question: What does the ref() function do in Vue 3?

answers.text: Creates a reactive reference to a value
Creates a reference to a DOM element

question: How do you access the value of a ref in the script?

ref is for objects; reactive is for primitives

question: What is the setup() function?

A lifecycle hook that runs after mount

question: What is Teleport in Vue 3?

A way to send data between components

question: What is a Fragment in Vue 3?

answers.text: Components can have multiple root nodes, no wrapping required

question: What is onMounted in Vue 3?

answers.text: A lifecycle hook registered inside setup() equivalent to mounted

answers.text: Using the computed() function inside setup()

question: How do you watch a reactive value in Vue 3?

A way to set up scripts

question: How do you define props in script setup?
How do you emit events in script setup?

provide/inject only works with Options API

answers.text: Using composable functions (custom hooks) for reusable logic
Mixins are still the only way

question: How do you define emits in Options API Vue 3?

answers.text: Using the emits option array or object

question: What is the defineComponent function?

A required function for all components

question: How do you bind multiple values with v-model in Vue 3?

question: What is the onUnmounted lifecycle hook?

Using ref() with a string argument

question: Can you use both Options API and Composition API in the same component?

answers.text: Yes, Vue 3 supports both APIs in the same component

answers.text: Global properties available to all components in the app

answers.text: Creates a reactive reference to a value

answers.text: Using the computed() function imported from Vue

A function to send data between components

question: What is toRefs in Vue 3?

question: What is watchEffect in Vue 3?

A more powerful version of watch

question: What is the difference between watch and watchEffect?

answers.text: watch watches specific sources; watchEffect auto-tracks dependencies
watch is async; watchEffect is sync
watch is for refs; watchEffect is for reactive

question: What is shallowRef in Vue 3?

A ref that is not reactive

question: What is shallowReactive in Vue 3?

A reactive object with no depth

question: What is triggerRef in Vue 3?

answers.text: Manually triggers effects associated with a shallowRef

question: What is customRef in Vue 3?

answers.text: Creates a ref with custom track and trigger behavior

question: What is readonly in Vue 3?

answers.text: Makes a reactive object or ref read-only

question: What is shallowReadonly in Vue 3?

answers.text: A read-only that is not reactive

question: What is isRef in Vue 3?

A function that checks if a ref exists
A method to check ref type

question: What is isReactive in Vue 3?

answers.text: A utility to check if an object is reactive

A method to check data binding

question: What is the new reactivity system in Vue 3?

answers.text: Uses JavaScript Proxy for more complete reactivity

question: What advantage does Vue 3 Proxy-based reactivity have?

answers.text: Detects new properties, array index changes, and more

question: How do you watch multiple sources in Vue 3?

question: What is the onBeforeMount hook in Vue 3?

answers.text: Called before the component is mounted to the DOM

question: What is the onBeforeUpdate hook?

Called before the component is created

question: What is the onUpdated hook?

answers.text: Called after the DOM has been updated

Called when the component is mounted

question: What is the onDeactivated hook?

answers.text: Called when a kept-alive component is deactivated

question: What is the onErrorCaptured hook?

answers.text: Called when an error is propagated from a descendant component

question: What is the onRenderTracked hook in Vue 3?

answers.text: Called when a reactive dependency is tracked during component render

question: What is the onRenderTriggered hook?

question: What are Symbols in JavaScript?

question: What is the difference between WeakMap and Map?

question: What are microtasks and macrotasks in the event loop?

question: What is the difference between Object.freeze() and Object.seal()?

question: What is a tail call optimization in JavaScript?

A technique for chaining function calls

question: What is the Temporal API (or Intl.DateTimeFormat)?

question: What is the module pattern in JavaScript?
